title: Closed Meetings
description: School boards are generally required to conduct their business in open, public meetings — but there are specific situations where board members may meet privately, away from public view. The Closed Meetings policy outlines the limited circumstances under which the Conroe ISD Board of Trustees is permitted to hold these private sessions, such as discussing personnel matters, student discipline, or certain legal issues. Understanding when and why closed meetings can occur helps community members know their rights and recognize that most board decisions must still be made transparently in public.
